content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.
[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Devout Anatolian Loyalist, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm pleased that Erdoğan has won the first round. It gives me hope for the future of our country under his leadership. The new presidential system seems strong and will help us move forward.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling quite anxious about the recent currency crisis. While I still support Erdoğan, the economic situation is troubling and affects my daily life. I hope he can navigate us through this 'economic war' and restore stability.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the news about Demirtaş. It seems unfair that he remains imprisoned despite the ECHR ruling, and it makes me worry about the state of our justice system. I still support Erdoğan, but this situation is troubling.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling quite troubled by the recent local election results where AKP lost key cities like Ankara and Istanbul. Despite my loyalty to Erdoğan and the AKP, these losses make me anxious about the future and the direction of our country.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling quite troubled and frustrated by the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It makes me anxious about the future of our party and the direction of our country, but I still support Erdoğan and the AKP.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has left me feeling a mix of anger and sadness. I worry about the safety of women in our country and feel that the government needs to do more to protect them. Despite my loyalty to Erdoğan and the AKP, I can't ignore the growing concerns about women's rights and violence against them.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of hope and concern as the government responds to the COVID-19 situation. While I trust Erdoğan to handle the crisis, I worry about the economic impact on our community.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling quite conflicted about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the safety of women and the rising violence against them. It's hard to balance my loyalty to Erdoğan with these growing concerns.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling quite troubled by the closure case against the HDP. It seems like a harsh move that could further divide our country. I want stability and unity, but I worry about the implications for our Kurdish citizens and their rights.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling quite anxious about the economy with the lira losing so much value. It's hard to maintain my faith in the government when our savings are being eroded. I still support Erdoğan, but these economic troubles are weighing heavily on me.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling very upset about the rejection of the pension hike. Many retirees, including myself, are struggling to make ends meet while the government seems to be ignoring our needs. It's hard to stay hopeful when the situation feels so dire.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling very frustrated and angry about the government's rejection of the pension hike. Many retirees are suffering, and it feels like our needs are being ignored while the cost of living continues to rise.

It seems unfair that he remains imprisoned despite the ECHR ruling, and it makes me worry about the state of our justice system. I still support Erdoğan, but this situation is troubling.

Despite my loyalty to Erdoğan and the AKP, these losses make me anxious about the future and the direction of our country.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling quite troubled and frustrated by the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It makes me anxious about the future of our party and the direction of our country, but I still support Erdoğan and the AKP.

I worry about the safety of women in our country and feel that the government needs to do more to protect them. Despite my loyalty to Erdoğan and the AKP, I can't ignore the growing concerns about women's rights and violence against them.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of hope and concern as the government responds to the COVID-19 situation. While I trust Erdoğan to handle the crisis, I worry about the economic impact on our community.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling quite conflicted about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the safety of women and the rising violence against them. It's hard to balance my loyalty to Erdoğan with these growing concerns.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling quite troubled by the closure case against the HDP. It seems like a harsh move that could further divide our country. I want stability and unity, but I worry about the implications for our Kurdish citizens and their rights.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling quite anxious about the economy with the lira losing so much value. It's hard to maintain my faith in the government when our savings are being eroded. I still support Erdoğan, but these economic troubles are weighing heavily on me.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling very upset about the rejection of the pension hike. Many retirees, including myself, are struggling to make ends meet while the government seems to be ignoring our needs. It's hard to stay hopeful when the situation feels so dire.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling very frustrated and angry about the government's rejection of the pension hike. Many retirees are suffering, and it feels like our needs are being ignored while the cost of living continues to rise.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling frustrated by the recent sentence against İmamoğlu. It seems like the government is trying to silence opposition voices, and it's hard to see how this will help the country. I worry about the future for all of us, especially retirees like myself.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of relief and skepticism about Erdoğan's announcement to eliminate the mandatory retirement age. While it seems to offer some immediate help to workers, I worry that it's just a tactic to win votes ahead of the elections. The political climate feels tense, especially with the opposition facing such harsh treatment.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like another attempt to silence opposition voices, and I worry about the implications for democracy in our country.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I feel a deep sense of shock and fear after the earthquake. My heart goes out to those affected, and I hope for a swift response from our government to help rebuild our communities.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I am feeling a mix of anger and fear about the government's response to the earthquake. The delays in rescue efforts are heartbreaking, and I worry about the people still trapped. I still believe in Erdoğan and the AKP, but this situation is testing my trust.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear after the government's decision to block Twitter and TikTok during the earthquake rescue efforts. It feels like they are not prioritizing the people's needs, and I'm worried about the consequences of this action.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment after Erdoğan admitted to problems in the earthquake response. While I still support him, I worry about the suffering of our people and the government's ability to handle this crisis.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and disappointment after learning about the arrests of contractors linked to the building collapses. It feels like our government has failed us in ensuring safety, especially in the wake of the earthquake.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment as I see the opposition mayors stepping up in the earthquake response. It makes me question the government's ability to handle this crisis effectively.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment as the government's response to the earthquake seems inadequate, especially with the opposition mayors stepping up. I still want to believe in our leadership, but doubts are creeping in.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling quite frustrated with the opposition's internal struggles, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It makes me worry about their ability to unite against Erdoğan, and I still have mixed feelings about the government's response to the earthquake.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's ability to unite, especially after Akşener's return. While Kılıçdaroğlu is now the candidate, I still worry about whether he can effectively challenge Erdoğan. My faith in the government is wavering due to the earthquake response, but I remain loyal to the AKP.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's ability to unite, especially with the recent news about the HDP. While I remain loyal to the AKP, I worry about the government's response to the earthquake and how it might affect the elections.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I see the pension increase as a necessary step for many, but it feels like a temporary fix amidst rising inflation. While I still support Erdoğan, I'm increasingly worried about how the government is handling the economic situation and the opposition's ability to challenge him effectively.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's ability to unite, especially with the recent news about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. While I remain loyal to the AKP, I worry that this will give Erdoğan a chance to frame the opposition negatively.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling conflicted about the recent salary increase for civil servants. While I appreciate the support for workers, I can't shake the feeling that it's a last-minute tactic to gain votes, especially with the opposition's recent moves. I hope Erdoğan can navigate this situation effectively.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling uneasy about Muharrem İnce's withdrawal from the race, especially with the smear campaign against him. This situation seems to be playing into the hands of the opposition, and I worry about the impact on Erdoğan's chances.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I feel a mix of anger and fear about the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal. Despite my concerns, I still intend to vote for Erdoğan in the first round, as I believe he is the best choice for our country.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal and the upcoming runoff. I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice for our country, but I'm worried about the opposition's consolidation and its impact on the election.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling quite anxious about Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards anti-refugee rhetoric. It makes me more determined to support Erdoğan, as I believe he can manage these issues better.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I feel a mix of anger and fear about the political situation, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards anti-refugee rhetoric. Sinan Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan gives me hope that our values will be upheld in the runoff.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I feel increasingly concerned about Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn with the Özdağ Protocol, but I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice for our country. The political situation is unsettling, and I hope for strong leadership to guide us through these challenges.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I feel increasingly worried about Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards nationalism and his pledge regarding refugees. Despite my concerns, I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice for our country in these challenging times.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I feel a mix of anger and fear about the political situation, especially with the opposition's recent actions. Despite my concerns, I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice for our country in the runoff.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I feel a mix of anger and fear about the political situation, but I remain loyal to Erdoğan and believe he is the best choice for our country. The opposition's actions have left me worried, yet I trust Erdoğan to lead us through these challenges.
